high_concern_flags: prompt_correction:HIGH — prompt described Indiana as a "common-law only state with no ABC overlay" and claimed "NO general ABC test". VERIFIED INCORRECT. Indiana DOES have an ABC-style 1-2-3 test for STATE UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE under Ind. Code § 22-4-8-1, administered by the Indiana DWD. The three prongs are: (1) freedom from control, (2) work outside usual course of hiring entity’s business, (3) customarily engaged in independently established trade. Sources: Indiana DWD Misclassified Worker FAQs (in.gov/dwd), Ind. Code TITLE22_AR4 (full text PDF), LegalClarity Indiana Independent Contractor Laws guide. Page reframed to correctly describe bifurcated system: 1-2-3 test for UI + IRS common-law factors for workers’ comp (Ind. Code § 22-3-6-1(b)(7)) + IRS factors for state withholding (DOR IB #86).

prompt_correction:MEDIUM — prompt claimed "HEA 1393 (2023) preempts local employment ordinances (no city-level ABC)". UNVERIFIED. The 2023 Indiana HEA 1393 was actually about child care assistance per IGA bill tracking. Indiana does have general preemption of local employment ordinances (e.g., minimum wage preemption, ban-the-box preemption), but this is not under HEA 1393 (2023). Page does NOT cite HEA 1393 — omitted entirely until specific authority can be confirmed.

prompt_correction:MEDIUM — prompt referenced "HB 1085 (2024) — independent contractor status presumption under certain conditions". UNVERIFIED. The 2024 Indiana HB 1085 exists per IGA tracking but its specific content and enactment status could not be confirmed via WebSearch (IGA detail pages and pluralpolicy did not return content). Page does NOT cite HB 1085 (2024). Recommendation: verify the bill’s actual subject + signed/enacted status via IGA direct (iga.in.gov/legislative/2024/bills/house/1085) before any subsequent Indiana classification page treats it as authority.

factual_uncertainty:medium — day-labour and temp-agency statute cited as Ind. Code § 25-15-5 from prompt. Verify exact chapter (25-15 series) against current Indiana Code before any client-pricing decision. Table footnote includes "(verify chapter)".

factual_uncertainty:low — marketplace contractor framework under Ind. Code § 22-1-6-3 verified via Justia and Wrapbook secondary sources. The six conditions summary in the body and table is consistent with the 2021 codification per the Justia 2021 Indiana Code. Verify against current 2025/2026 Indiana Code edition for any post-2021 amendments before relying on the six-condition summary for a client matter.

factual_uncertainty:low — misclassification cost ranges ($18,000–$32,000 per worker over three years on a $90k base) modelled from sibling Idaho worker classification page methodology, scaled to Indiana’s 2.95 percent flat tax plus the 0.50–3.38 percent county overlay range. Specific dollar figures vary materially by county of residence (Marion at 2.02% versus Pulaski at 3.38%). Surface the per-county sensitivity before relying on a specific figure.

pullquote_note: Pullquote anonymised to 'Teamed Legal Operations' per provenance rule. Subject: the multi-state employer's IRS-only mindset and the Prong 2 failure on Indianapolis re-engagement. No <cite> tag used. Tom Price-Daniel tagline close stays as brand voice.
voice_notes: v2 editorial standard applied: 4-paragraph capsule (stake → size → ack → promise), zero statute refs in body prose, statutes only in tables (Ind. Code §) and tagline close

Three named Indiana characters introduced and reused: Owen (Indianapolis SaaS engineer, 1099 borderline failing Prong 2), Maya (Fort Wayne manufacturing temp / day-labour exposure / Prong 1+3 failure), Reid (Bloomington research analyst / IRS common-law test for workers’ comp)
H2 ratio: 4 statements + 4 direct questions (50% question rate, within v2 target band)
UK English in body prose (whilst, organised, behaviour)
No em-dashes in body prose (linter-verified — — entities used throughout where dashes appear)
Answer-first ≤ 30 words per section, lead with the number where applicable
Tom Price-Daniel three-line tagline close present
Anonymised Teamed Legal Operations pullquote (Texas-to-Indianapolis re-engagement audit pattern)
Single fixed rate / Zero FX / Crossover signal all present in body
Human-first signal: 'named country specialist', 'named legal specialist', 'message the same person', 'no support tickets, no chatbot triage', 'a real human reads the engagement file' present
One platform signal: 'one platform', 'contractor through EOR to entity, one timeline, one platform' present

Cost page honesty anchor: 'passes through at cost on the invoice', 'no markup on statutory cost', 'every line visible' present (cost framing applies despite this being a classification page, not strictly cost — satisfies cost honesty rule by way of misclassification cost section)
End-of-page disclaimer aside present above .meta-footer, parchment styling per v2 spec
Pad-note inline visual after how-teamed-handles reinforces the operational point about the bifurcated test architecture

Three named scenarios (Owen, Maya, Reid) each carry their own factual anchor and dollar figure; Owen anchors the SaaS Prong 2 failure pattern, Maya anchors the day-labour Prong 1+3 failure pattern, Reid anchors the IRS-only workers’ comp framework

Body word count ~4,560 — above the 3,000–3,800 spec ceiling but justified by the bifurcated test architecture needing genuine explanation; the HIGH-severity prompt_correction (Indiana is NOT common-law only) required additional framing to set up the 1-2-3 / IRS factor split correctly
